column1: With the help of the Slave Woman, Mayzun, you found the Emir Ali hiding in his bedroom.  With your urging (and a few sharp remarks from Mayzun), the Emir swore he'd restore the city.  By the first light of dawn, the Emir Ali al-Din Hasan ordered all fighting to cease and proclaimed the evil rule of Ad Avis and Khaveen to be over.  As the Emir began an incredibly dull speech, you slipped away to complete your quest.
column2: With the help of the Slave Woman, Mayzun, you found the Emir Ali hiding in his bedroom.  With your urging (and a few sharp remarks from Mayzun), the Emir swore he'd restore the city.  By the first light of dawn, the Emir Ali al-Din Hasan ordered all fighting to cease and proclaimed the evil rule of Ad Avis and Khaveen to be over.  As the Emir began an incredibly dull speech, you slipped away to complete your quest.

column1: You stare in wonder at the powerful transformation of the Plaza by the magic of the Water Elemental.
column2: You stare in wonder at the powerful transformation of the Plaza by the magic of the Water Elemental.

column1: Will anyone speak for this Stranger from the North?
column2: Will anyone speak for this Stranger from the North?

column1: We are Shameen and Shema, keepers of the Katta's Tail Inn.  When the Katta were driven from Raseir, we journeyed to the Northland in search of a hero.
column2: We are Shameen and Shema, keepers of the Katta's Tail Inn.  When the Katta were driven from Raseir, we journeyed to the Northland in search of a hero.

column1: He journeyed back with us to Shapeir.  Here he defeated the Elementals of Fire, Air, Earth, and Water to save our city.
column2: He journeyed back with us to Shapeir.  Here he defeated the Elementals of Fire, Air, Earth, and Water to save our city.

column1: Who will speak for this man of compassion?
column2: Who will speak for this man of compassion?

column1: Who will speak for this man of Honor?
column2: Who will speak for this man of Honor?

column1: I am Zayishah, daughter of the Emir Ali Al-Din Hasan.
column2: I am Zayishah, daughter of the Emir Ali Al-Din Hasan.

column1: I am the Poet Omar, Teller of tales for all to hear.
column2: I am the Poet Omar, Teller of tales for all to hear.

column1: I proclaim this man a Hero,
column2: I proclaim this man a Hero,

column1: Honesty in dealing with others, and Mercy to his enemies.
column2: Honesty in dealing with others, and Mercy to his enemies.

column1: He is a man of great Honor.
column2: He is a man of great Honor.

column1: CONGRATULATIONS!! You have successfully completed "Quest for Glory II:  Trial by Fire" with the maximum possible score, %d of %d!! We welcome you to the ranks of the few, the proud, the True Heroes!

column1: Ha Ha Ha Haaaaaaah Ha Haah Ha Ha Haaaah Ha Haaaa!
column2: Ha Ha Ha Haaaaaaah Ha Haah Ha Ha Haaaah Ha Haaaa!

column1: And so, as the moon moves slowly through the starry sky above the mountains and the desert of Shapeir, our Hero bids a fond "Adieu" to his latest set of adventures and miserable puns.  Where will the road lead next...?
column2: And so, as the moon moves slowly through the starry sky above the mountains and the desert of Shapeir, our Hero bids a fond "Adieu" to his latest set of adventures and miserable puns.  Where will the road lead next...?

column1: But wait!  There's more to come in the next thrilling saga of "Quest for Glory" by Lori and Corey.
column2: But wait!  There's more to come in the next thrilling saga of "Quest for Glory" by Lori and Corey.

column1: The best way to get goods across the desert is by pack saurus.
column2: The best way to get goods across the desert is by pack saurus.

column1: The Bedouins of the desert are good people, for the most part.
column2: The Bedouins of the desert are good people, for the most part.

column1: Before the sun has risen over the mountaintops, you are awakened by Shameen, who tells you that even now, the caravan to Raseir is preparing to depart.
column2: Before the sun has risen over the mountaintops, you are awakened by Shameen, who tells you that even now, the caravan to Raseir is preparing to depart.

column1: Shema greets you as you leave the room with some breakfast and supplies to take with you.  The inn is filled with katta merchants who wish you luck upon your departure.
column2: Shema greets you as you leave the room with some breakfast and supplies to take with you.  The inn is filled with katta merchants who wish you luck upon your departure.

column1: As you pass through the gate of Shapeir, the gate guard presents you with a new saurus, a gift from the Sultan.  Ahead in the desert sands awaits a mass of Saurii and riders being led by the Caravan Master, a huge Centaur who orders the Caravan into motion before the sun can be seen.
column2: As you pass through the gate of Shapeir, the gate guard presents you with a new saurus, a gift from the Sultan.  Ahead in the desert sands awaits a mass of Saurii and riders being led by the Caravan Master, a huge Centaur who orders the Caravan into motion before the sun can be seen.

column1: The only way of getting goods across the desert is by pack saurus.
column2: The only way of getting goods across the desert is by pack saurus.

column1: The caravan comes under siege from hundreds of nomadic brigands.  Your small group is vastly outnumbered.  The situation looks grim.
column2: The caravan comes under siege from hundreds of nomadic brigands.  Your small group is vastly outnumbered.  The situation looks grim.

column1: I love the smell of victory in the morning.      ......  It smells like.....sweat.. no, no      ...oregano ....nooo...like Victory!  Yea      Victory!...Yea! Yea!  That's the ticket.
column2: I love the smell of victory in the morning.      ......  It smells like.....sweat.. no, no      ...oregano ....nooo...like Victory!  Yea      Victory!...Yea! Yea!  That's the ticket.

column1: After a week of travelling, you leave the Caravan when it comes within sight of the city of Raseir.
column2: After a week of travelling, you leave the Caravan when it comes within sight of the city of Raseir.

column1: He rears up and salutes you, and you watch the Caravan move onward into the desert.
column2: He rears up and salutes you, and you watch the Caravan move onward into the desert.

column1: The saurus is an amazing beast.  This steed must have remained at the gate, awaiting your future need, while you carried on your business in Raseir.
column2: The saurus is an amazing beast.  This steed must have remained at the gate, awaiting your future need, while you carried on your business in Raseir.

column1: Defining polygons (or paths):  Move cursor to point and click button to define point. Ctrl click marks path end.  Other useful keys:  F4 or <alt-C>: Show control screen. F2 or <alt-V>: Show visual. E: toggle ego showing or hidden. P: toggle position info on/off. <alt-M>: Modify path. <ctrl-S>: Quit and save all path data ESC: Quit without saving.
column2: Defining polygons (or paths):  Move cursor to point and click button to define point. Ctrl click marks path end.  Other useful keys:  F4 or <alt-C>: Show control screen. column1: Modifying defined polygons:  <space>: Move to next defined point. <enter>: Select and edit point. C: Cut point. I: Insert point after current point. ESC: Return to creating new points. F4 or <alt-C>: Show control screen. F2 or <alt-V>: Show visual. E: toggle ego showing or hidden. P: toggle position info on/off.  Editing point:  Move cursor to new point and click mouse button.
